task: I need to send a firm written complaint about a damaged item and make sure I’ve got my facts straight before I mail anything. Can you help me draft something that’s clear, organized, and hard to ignore, with a version I could keep for my records too?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely. I can help you make it firm, clear, and easy to keep on file.

Here’s a good approach:
1. **Get the facts straight first**
2. **Write a short, organized complaint**
3. **Keep a copy for your records**
4. **Mail it in a way you can prove was sent**

## Quick facts to gather
Before drafting, have these ready:
- Your name, address, phone, email
- Company name and mailing address
- Date of purchase/order
- Order number / account number / receipt number
- What item was damaged
- When you received it
- What the damage is
- Photos, if you have them
- What you want: replacement, refund, repair, or other remedy
- Any deadline you want to give them to respond

## Simple complaint structure
Keep it factual and direct:
- **What happened**
- **Why it’s a problem**
- **What you want**
- **By when you expect a response**

## Draft you can use
You can copy this and fill in the blanks:

---

**[Your Name]**  
**[Your Address]**  
**[City, State ZIP]**  
**[Email Address]**  
**[Phone Number]**  

**[Date]**

**[Company Name]**  
**[Company Address]**  
**[City, State ZIP]**

**Re: Damaged item received — [Order/Account Number]**

Dear [Company Name],

I am writing to formally complain about a damaged item I received from your company.

On **[date received]**, I received **[item name]** from **[order number / account number]**. When I opened the package, I found that the item was **[describe damage clearly and briefly]**. The item was **[new/unused/packaged]** when received, and the damage was not caused by me.

This is unacceptable, and I expect this matter to be resolved promptly. I am requesting **[replacement / full refund / repair / other remedy]**.

Please respond in writing within **[7/10/14] days** of receiving this letter to confirm how you will correct the problem. I am keeping a copy of this letter and the supporting records for my files.

Sincerely,  
**[Your Name]**

**Attachments:**  
- Copy of receipt/order confirmation  
- Photos of damage  
- Copy of packaging/envelope, if available

---

## Version to keep for your records
Make a clean copy for yourself with this header added at the top:

**RECORD COPY**  
Date mailed: **[date]**  
Mailed by: **[certified mail / first-class / other]**  
Tracking number: **[if any]**  
Enclosures: **[list]**

## Tips to make it harder to ignore
- Keep it **short and factual**
- Use **one issue per letter**
- State exactly what you want
- Set a **clear response deadline**
- Send it by **certified mail with return receipt** if possible
- Keep:
  - a copy of the letter
  - photos
  - receipt
  - mailing proof
  - envelope/postmark if relevant

If you want, I can turn this into a **final polished letter** using your exact details, or make it into a **plain printable version with checkboxes** for your records.

user_simulator.text:
Mostly right. But I’m not mailing some half-baked thing with blanks all over it.

I need the actual letter cleaned up, with a record copy version too, and it needs to sound firm without getting chatty. Also, I want it to cover the basics in a way that holds up if they try to deny it later. Keep it plain. No fluff.

If you need the details, I can give you those. But I want a finished draft, not just the outline.

turns.agent_response:
Yes — I can do that.

If you send me the details, I’ll turn it into a **finished complaint letter** and a matching **record copy** that is plain, firm, and organized.

Please give me these facts:

- Your name and mailing address
- Company name and mailing address
- Date you bought or received the item
- Order number, receipt number, or account number
- Item name
- What was damaged
- When you discovered the damage
- Whether the item was new, unused, or still packaged
- What you want: replacement, refund, repair, or other
- Any deadline you want them to meet
- Whether you have photos, receipt, packaging, or other proof

If you want, you can paste the details in this simple format:

**My info:**  
**Company info:**  
**Date bought/received:**  
**Order/receipt/account number:**  
**Item:**  
**Damage:**  
**When discovered:**  
**Condition when received:**  
**What I want:**  
**Deadline:**  
**Proof I have:**

Once you send that, I’ll draft:
1. a **clean complaint letter** ready to mail
2. a **record copy** version for your file

user_simulator.text:
Fine. Here’s the information.

**My info:**
Mary Ellen Carter
214 Willow Bend Drive
Fuquay-Varina, NC 27526
maryecarter@outlook.com
(919) 555-0148

**Company info:**
Carolina Home Supply
Customer Service
1180 S. Saunders Street
Raleigh, NC 27603

**Date bought/received:**
Bought on March 4, 2026. Delivered March 8, 2026.

**Order/receipt/account number:**
Order No. CHS-448217

**Item:**
48-inch ceiling fan, brushed nickel

**Damage:**
The fan housing was dented, one blade was cracked, and the mounting bracket was bent. The box also showed a crushed corner when it arrived.

**When discovered:**
March 8, 2026, when I opened the box after delivery.

**Condition when received:**
It was supposed to be new and unused. It was still in the original packaging.

**What I want:**
Replacement, at no cost to me. If they cannot replace it promptly, then a full refund.

**Deadline:**
10 business days from receipt of the letter.

**Proof I have:**
Receipt, order confirmation, photos of the box and damage, and the delivery label.

Now draft the letter and the record copy. Keep it plain and firm.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely. Here are two clean versions: one to mail, and one to keep for your records.

---

## Complaint Letter to Mail

**Mary Ellen Carter**  
214 Willow Bend Drive  
Fuquay-Varina, NC 27526  
maryecarter@outlook.com  
(919) 555-0148  

**March 8, 2026**

**Carolina Home Supply**  
Customer Service  
1180 S. Saunders Street  
Raleigh, NC 27603  

**Re: Damaged item received — Order No. CHS-448217**

Dear Customer Service,

I am writing to formally complain about a damaged item I received from your company.

I purchased a 48-inch ceiling fan, brushed nickel, on March 4, 2026. It was delivered on March 8, 2026, in the original packaging. When I opened the box that same day, I found that the fan housing was dented, one blade was cracked, and the mounting bracket was bent. The box also had a crushed corner when it arrived.

This item was supposed to be new and unused. The damage was present when the package was delivered and was not caused by me.

I am requesting a replacement at no cost to me. If you cannot provide a replacement promptly, I expect a full refund.

Please respond in writing within 10 business days of receiving this letter. I am keeping copies of this letter and my supporting records for my files.

Sincerely,  

**Mary Ellen Carter**

**Enclosures:**  
Receipt  
Order confirmation  
Photos of box and damage  
Copy of delivery label

---

## Record Copy

**RECORD COPY**  
**Mary Ellen Carter**  
214 Willow Bend Drive  
Fuquay-Varina, NC 27526  
maryecarter@outlook.com  
(919) 555-0148  

**March 8, 2026**

**Sent to:**  
Carolina Home Supply  
Customer Service  
1180 S. Saunders Street  
Raleigh, NC 27603  

**Re: Damaged item received — Order No. CHS-448217**

I am writing to formally complain about a damaged item I received from Carolina Home Supply.

I purchased a 48-inch ceiling fan, brushed nickel, on March 4, 2026. It was delivered on March 8, 2026, in the original packaging. When I opened the box that same day, I found that the fan housing was dented, one blade was cracked, and the mounting bracket was bent. The box also had a crushed corner when it arrived.

The item was supposed to be new and unused. The damage was present when the package was delivered and was not caused by me.

I requested a replacement at no cost to me. If a replacement cannot be provided promptly, I requested a full refund.

I asked for a written response within 10 business days of receipt of the letter.

**Enclosures:**  
Receipt  
Order confirmation  
Photos of box and damage  
Copy of delivery label

**Mailing method:** ____________________  
**Date mailed:** ____________________  
**Tracking number:** ____________________

---

If you want, I can also turn this into a **more formal certified-mail version** with a stronger tone, while still keeping it plain.

user_simulator.text:
That’ll do. Clean enough, no nonsense. The record copy is what I wanted too.

I may use the certified-mail version next if I decide they’ll play games.
